page: Amazon Web Services g4dn.8xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) running at 2.5 GHz, built on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

Acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA T4 GPU from the Turing family, featuring 16 GB of VRAM. For local storage, the instance includes a 900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps, making it optimized for network and EBS throughput.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear division between CPU-only tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. Single-core CPU performance is categorized as weak, with stress-ng single-core and gzip compression scoring in the bottom 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and general database operations fall into the average tier. However, the hardware excels in machine learning tasks; LLM inference speed for prompt processing and text generation using medium (7B) models ranks in the top tier (top 10%), while small (135M) model text generation is strong.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g4dn.8xlarge offers a balanced entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads, pairing dedicated Intel Xeon CPU resources with a dedicated NVIDIA T4 accelerator. This hardware profile is best suited for machine learning inference, graphics-intensive applications, video transcoding, and workloads requiring high network throughput alongside local NVMe SSD storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.8xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, it features 32 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a single NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. The instance includes a 900 GB NVMe SSD local storage volume and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark data shows weak single-core CPU performance but average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. Notably, the instance achieves top-tier performance in LLM inference for small and medium models, particularly in prompt processing and text generation. This resource profile makes the g4dn.8xlarge highly suitable for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and network-optimized applications requiring local SSD storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.8xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, a 16 GB NVIDIA T4 GPU, and 900 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for machine learning inference and graphics workloads.

tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, an NVIDIA T4 GPU, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for inference workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 16 GB NVIDIA T4 GPU
Includes 900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier performance in LLM inference for small and medium-sized models
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Suited for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and video transcoding
